properties.name: Palisades Fire
properties.description: Devastating wildfire that swept through Pacific Palisades and surrounding neighborhoods in January 2025, destroying thousands of structures in one of Los Angeles' most destructive fire events.

properties.name: Eaton Fire
properties.description: Destructive wildfire that burned through Altadena and the foothills above Pasadena in January 2025, destroying over 7,000 structures.

properties.name: Jasper Wildfire
properties.description: Massive wildfire that swept into the town of Jasper in July 2024, destroying roughly a third of the town's structures within Jasper National Park.

properties.name: Park Fire
properties.description: California's fourth-largest wildfire in state history, burning across Butte and Tehama counties from July to August 2024.

properties.name: Maui Wildfires (Lahaina Fire)
properties.description: Deadliest US wildfire in over a century. Wind-driven fire devastated the historic town of Lahaina on August 8, 2023, destroying nearly 2,200 structures.

properties.name: 2023 Canadian Wildfire Season
properties.region: Multiple provinces (Quebec, BC, Alberta, NWT, Ontario)
properties.description: Record-breaking Canadian wildfire season that burned over 45 million acres, more than double any previously recorded season. Smoke blanketed much of North America.

properties.name: Central Chile Wildfires
properties.description: Devastating wildfires in south-central Chile during the 2023 summer, killing at least 26 people and destroying thousands of homes.

properties.name: Hermits Peak / Calf Canyon Fire
properties.description: Largest wildfire in New Mexico history, caused by a US Forest Service prescribed burn that escaped control. Burned from April to August 2022.

properties.name: Dixie Fire
properties.description: Largest single (non-complex) wildfire in California history. Burned for over three months and destroyed the town of Greenville.

properties.name: 2021 Turkey Wildfires
properties.region: Antalya and Muğla provinces, Mediterranean coast
properties.description: Turkey's worst wildfire season on record. Over 200 fires broke out across southern Turkey in July–August 2021 during an extreme heatwave.

properties.name: 2020 US West Coast Fire Siege
properties.description: Record-breaking wildfire season across the US West Coast. Simultaneous large fires in California, Oregon, and Washington burned millions of acres and created hazardous smoke conditions.

properties.name: Pantanal Fires
properties.region: Pantanal wetlands, Mato Grosso do Sul
properties.description: Record fires burned roughly 30% of the world's largest tropical wetland, the Pantanal, devastating wildlife habitat.

properties.name: Black Summer Bushfires
properties.region: New South Wales, Victoria, South Australia, Queensland
properties.description: Catastrophic bushfire season from September 2019 to March 2020 across southeastern Australia. Killed an estimated 3 billion animals and destroyed over 3,000 homes.

properties.name: 2019 Amazon Rainforest Fires
properties.description: Surge of fires in the Amazon in August 2019, driven by deforestation and drought. Over 80,000 fires detected by satellite, drawing global attention.

properties.name: 2019 Siberian Wildfires
properties.description: Massive wildfires across Siberia's boreal forest in summer 2019, releasing large amounts of CO₂ and blanketing cities in smoke.

properties.name: Camp Fire
properties.description: Deadliest and most destructive wildfire in California history. Destroyed the town of Paradise on November 8, 2018, killing 85 people and destroying 18,804 structures.

properties.name: Attica Wildfires
properties.description: Deadly wildfire swept through the seaside village of Mati near Athens on July 23, 2018. Rapid spread trapped residents trying to escape to the sea.

properties.name: Pedrógão Grande Fire
properties.description: Deadliest wildfire in Portuguese history. Many victims were trapped in their cars on a road between Figueiró dos Vinhos and Castanheira de Pêra on June 17, 2017.

properties.name: October 2017 Portugal Wildfires
properties.description: Series of devastating wildfires across central and northern Portugal in October 2017, fueled by Hurricane Ophelia's dry winds. Combined with the June fires, 2017 was Portugal's deadliest fire year.

properties.name: Fort McMurray Wildfire (The Beast)
properties.description: Costliest disaster in Canadian history (~$9.9 billion). Forced evacuation of all 88,000 residents of Fort McMurray in May 2016 and destroyed 2,400 structures.

properties.name: 2015 Indonesian Forest Fires
properties.description: Widespread peat and forest fires caused by El Niño drought and land-clearing practices. Generated a toxic haze that blanketed Southeast Asia for months.

properties.name: 2014 Northwest Territories Fires
properties.description: Record-breaking fire season in the Northwest Territories with over 385 fires burning 3.4 million hectares of boreal forest.

properties.name: 2010 Russian Wildfires
properties.description: Record heatwave fueled hundreds of wildfires across western Russia. Moscow was blanketed in thick smoke for weeks. Contributed to an estimated 56,000 excess deaths from heat and smoke.

properties.name: Black Saturday Bushfires
properties.description: Deadliest bushfire disaster in Australian history. On February 7, 2009, temperatures exceeded 46°C with extreme winds, igniting multiple fires that destroyed 2,029 homes.
properties.source: CFA Victoria / 2009 Victorian Bushfires Royal Commission

properties.name: 2007 Greek Forest Fires
properties.description: Series of devastating fires across Greece in August 2007, the worst in Greek history at the time. The Peloponnese was hardest hit with entire villages destroyed.

properties.name: 2003 Canberra Bushfires
properties.description: Bushfires reached the western suburbs of Canberra on January 18, 2003, destroying nearly 500 homes. A rare fire-generated tornado was observed.

properties.name: 2000 Montana Fire Season
properties.description: One of the worst US fire seasons in 50 years. Over 8.4 million acres burned nationally, with Montana and neighboring states bearing the brunt.

properties.name: 1997-98 Indonesian/Borneo Fires
properties.description: Catastrophic fires during the 1997–98 El Niño, burning massive areas of peatland and rainforest. Haze engulfed Southeast Asia, causing health and economic crises.

properties.name: Oakland Hills Firestorm
properties.description: The Tunnel Fire killed 25 people and destroyed over 3,000 homes in the Oakland and Berkeley hills on October 20, 1991, making it one of the worst urban-wildland fires in US history.

properties.name: Yellowstone Fires
properties.description: Largest fire complex in Yellowstone's history. Nearly 36% of the park burned in a series of fires from June through November 1988, transforming US fire management policy.

properties.name: Ash Wednesday Bushfires
properties.description: Over 180 fires broke out across Victoria and South Australia on February 16, 1983 in extreme conditions. One of Australia's deadliest bushfire events before Black Saturday.

properties.name: Black Dragon Fire
properties.description: Massive wildfire in northeastern China and into the Soviet Union in May 1987. One of the largest fires of the 20th century, burning 18 million acres of boreal forest.

properties.name: Sycamore Fire
properties.description: Fast-moving wildfire driven by Sundowner winds burned into the city of Santa Barbara in July 1977, destroying over 200 homes.

properties.name: Rio Grande do Sul Floods
properties.description: Worst flooding disaster in the state's history. Persistent heavy rains from late April through May 2024 submerged Porto Alegre and surrounding areas, displacing over 600,000 people.

properties.name: UAE / Dubai Floods
properties.description: Record rainfall of 254mm in 24 hours hit the UAE on April 16, 2024 — the heaviest in 75 years. Dubai's airport was flooded and roads became rivers.

properties.name: Libya Derna Floods (Storm Daniel)
properties.description: Storm Daniel brought extreme rainfall to eastern Libya on September 10, 2023. Two dams above Derna collapsed, sending a wall of water through the city and washing entire neighborhoods into the sea.

properties.name: 2022 Pakistan Floods
properties.description: Unprecedented monsoon rains and glacial melt flooded a third of Pakistan from June to October 2022, displacing 33 million people. Attributed partly to climate change.

properties.name: Western European Floods
properties.severity: Severe — deadliest European flood in decades
properties.description: Intense rainfall from storm Bernd caused catastrophic flooding in western Germany and eastern Belgium on July 14–15, 2021. The Ahr Valley was devastated with entire towns destroyed.

properties.name: Henan Province Floods
properties.severity: Extreme — year's worth of rain in 3 days
properties.description: Record-breaking rainfall hit Zhengzhou and Henan province in July 2021, with 201.9mm falling in a single hour. The Zhengzhou metro was flooded, trapping passengers.

properties.name: 2020 China Yangtze River Floods
properties.region: Yangtze River Basin, Hubei, Jiangxi, Anhui
properties.description: Prolonged heavy monsoon rains caused severe flooding along the Yangtze River from June to August 2020, affecting 63 million people and causing $25 billion in damage.

properties.name: 2019 Iran Floods
properties.description: Massive flooding across Iran from March to April 2019 after record rainfall. Flash floods devastated cities including Shiraz, and floodwaters persisted for weeks in Khuzestan.

properties.name: Kerala Floods
properties.severity: Worst in a century — all 14 districts affected
properties.description: Unusually heavy monsoon rainfall and dam releases caused catastrophic flooding across Kerala in August 2018, the worst in the state since 1924. Over 1.4 million people displaced.

properties.name: 2017 South Asian Floods (Bangladesh)
properties.description: Monsoon flooding in 2017 submerged roughly a third of Bangladesh, affecting over 8 million people. Simultaneous flooding hit Nepal and India's Bihar and Assam states.

properties.name: 2016 Louisiana Floods
properties.description: Slow-moving low-pressure system dropped up to 31 inches of rain over the Baton Rouge area in August 2016, flooding 146,000 homes. Called the worst US disaster since Hurricane Sandy.

properties.name: Tbilisi Flood
properties.description: Flash flooding and mudslides along the Vere River devastated parts of Tbilisi on June 13–14, 2015. The Tbilisi Zoo was destroyed and many animals escaped into the streets.

properties.name: Kashmir Floods
properties.description: Heavy monsoon rains caused devastating floods across Jammu & Kashmir in September 2014. Srinagar was inundated, with the Jhelum River reaching record levels.

properties.name: Uttarakhand Flash Floods
properties.description: Catastrophic flash floods and landslides struck the Uttarakhand region on June 16–17, 2013 during monsoon season. The Hindu pilgrimage site of Kedarnath was devastated.

properties.name: 2011 Thailand Floods
properties.region: Central Thailand, Chao Phraya River Basin, Bangkok
properties.description: Worst flooding in Thailand since 1942. Monsoon rains flooded the Chao Phraya River basin from July to January 2012, inundating industrial estates and parts of Bangkok. Cost $46 billion.

properties.name: 2010 Pakistan Floods
properties.region: Indus River Basin — KP, Punjab, Sindh, Balochistan
properties.description: Exceptional monsoon rains caused the Indus River to flood along almost its entire length. Approximately 20 million people were affected across 20% of Pakistan's land area.

properties.name: 2010-11 Queensland Floods
properties.severity: Severe — 3/4 of Queensland declared disaster zone
properties.description: Intense La Niña-driven rainfall caused widespread flooding across Queensland from December 2010 to January 2011. Flash floods devastated Toowoomba, and the Brisbane River flooded the CBD.

properties.name: 2005 Mumbai Floods
properties.description: Mumbai received 944mm of rainfall in 24 hours on July 26, 2005, one of the highest ever recorded in India. The city was paralyzed for days with massive loss of life and property.

properties.name: 1998 China Yangtze River Floods
properties.region: Yangtze River Basin, Hubei, Hunan, Jiangxi
properties.description: Massive flooding along the Yangtze River during the 1998 monsoon season affected 223 million people. Attributed to heavy rains combined with deforestation and wetland loss.

properties.name: Great Flood of 1993
properties.region: Mississippi and Missouri River Basins, Midwest
properties.description: Among the most costly and devastating floods in US history. The Mississippi and Missouri rivers flooded across nine states from April to October 1993, covering 30,000 square miles.

properties.name: 1991 Bangladesh Cyclone Flood
properties.description: Cyclone 02B made landfall on April 29, 1991, generating a 6-meter storm surge that inundated Bangladesh's southeastern coast, killing approximately 138,000 people.

properties.name: 2002 European Floods
properties.description: Extreme rainfall caused catastrophic flooding along the Elbe and Danube rivers in August 2002, devastating Prague, Dresden, and other cities. Damage exceeded €20 billion.

properties.name: 2000 Mozambique Floods
properties.description: Cyclone Eline and weeks of heavy rain caused massive flooding across southern Mozambique in February–March 2000, displacing hundreds of thousands. Images of helicopter rescues drew global attention.

properties.name: 1988 Bangladesh Floods
properties.description: Monsoon flooding submerged roughly 60% of Bangladesh from August to September 1988, including much of the capital Dhaka. Over 30 million people were affected.

properties.name: Typhoon Nina / Banqiao Dam Failure
properties.description: Typhoon Nina stalled over Henan Province in August 1975, dropping a year's worth of rain in 24 hours. The Banqiao Dam and 62 other dams failed, releasing a massive flood wave.

properties.name: Hurricane Harvey Floods
properties.severity: Catastrophic — 60+ inches of rain in some areas
properties.description: Hurricane Harvey stalled over the Houston area from August 25–30, 2017, dumping over 60 inches of rain in some areas. One of the costliest US natural disasters at $125 billion.

properties.name: Hurricane Mitch Floods
properties.description: Hurricane Mitch stalled off Honduras in October 1998, dumping torrential rain that caused catastrophic flooding and mudslides. Deadliest Atlantic hurricane since 1780 at the time.

properties.name: 2024 Amazon Drought
properties.severity: Worst recorded — rivers at historic lows
properties.description: Extreme drought dried up rivers across the Amazon basin in late 2024, stranding communities, killing river dolphins, and fueling record wildfires. The Rio Negro at Manaus fell to its lowest level ever recorded.

properties.name: Horn of Africa Drought (2020-2023)
properties.severity: Worst in 40 years — 5 consecutive failed rainy seasons
properties.description: Five consecutive failed rainy seasons from 2020 to 2023 created the worst drought in 40 years across the Horn of Africa. An estimated 43,000 excess deaths in Somalia alone in 2022.

properties.name: 2022 European Drought
properties.description: Europe experienced its worst drought in at least 500 years in the summer of 2022. Rivers dried up, crop yields fell sharply, and energy production was curtailed across the continent.

properties.name: Madagascar Drought
properties.description: Severe drought in southern Madagascar from 2020 to 2021 left over 1 million people food insecure, with some areas approaching famine. The first climate-change-driven famine according to some reports.

properties.name: 2019 India Drought
properties.severity: Severe — delayed monsoon, Chennai water crisis
properties.description: Delayed monsoon and below-normal rainfall led to severe drought across much of India in 2019. Chennai nearly ran out of water, and reservoirs across Maharashtra hit critically low levels.

properties.name: Cape Town Water Crisis
properties.description: Three years of below-average rainfall brought Cape Town close to 'Day Zero' — when taps would run dry — in early 2018. Strict water rationing of 50 liters per person per day was imposed.

properties.name: 2015-16 Southern Africa Drought
properties.country: South Africa / Zimbabwe / Malawi / Zambia / Mozambique
properties.severity: Worst in 35 years — El Niño driven
properties.description: Strong El Niño caused the worst drought in southern Africa since 1982-83. Over 40 million people faced food insecurity across the region. Maize production fell drastically.

properties.name: 2015-16 Ethiopian Drought
properties.region: Northern and Eastern Ethiopia (Tigray, Amhara, Oromia, Somali)
properties.severity: Worst in 50 years — 10+ million people food insecure
properties.description: El Niño-driven drought caused crop failures across Ethiopia in 2015-16. Over 10 million people required emergency food assistance, the country's worst food crisis in decades.

properties.name: 2012 United States Drought
properties.region: Central US — Great Plains, Midwest, South
properties.severity: Worst since 1956 — 65% of contiguous US in drought
properties.description: The most extensive drought in the US since the 1950s. By July 2012, 65% of the contiguous US was in drought. Massive crop losses led to $30 billion in agricultural damage.

properties.name: 2011 East Africa Drought
properties.severity: Worst in 60 years — famine declared in Somalia
properties.description: Severe drought caused a food crisis affecting over 13 million people. Famine was declared in parts of southern Somalia — the first famine declaration anywhere in the world since 1992.

properties.name: 2010 Amazon Drought
properties.severity: Worst on record at the time
properties.description: The 2010 Amazon drought was more severe than the previous record in 2005. The Rio Negro at Manaus fell to its lowest level in over a century, and millions of fish died.

properties.name: Millennium Drought (Australia)
properties.description: The Millennium Drought (1997–2009) was the worst drought in Australia's recorded history. 2006 was the peak year, with the Murray-Darling Basin experiencing its lowest inflows on record.

properties.name: 2002 India Drought
properties.region: Rajasthan, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh
properties.description: India experienced its worst drought in 15 years due to a major monsoon failure in 2002. Over 300 million people were affected, with severe impacts on agriculture and water supply.

properties.name: 1998 Southern US Drought
properties.description: Severe drought and heatwave gripped the southern United States in 1998, causing massive agricultural losses and contributing to over 200 heat-related deaths in Texas alone.

properties.name: 1991-92 Southern Africa Drought
properties.country: Zimbabwe / Mozambique / Zambia / South Africa / Malawi
properties.severity: Worst in 20th century for the region
properties.description: El Niño-driven drought devastated southern Africa in 1991–92. Maize production fell by 60% across the region, and over 20 million people faced severe food shortages.

properties.name: 1983-85 Ethiopian Famine Drought
properties.description: Prolonged drought from 1983 to 1985 caused one of the worst famines in modern history. An estimated 400,000 to 1 million people died. Prompted the Live Aid concert and global aid response.

properties.name: 1988 US Drought
properties.severity: Worst since Dust Bowl — $40 billion damage
properties.description: Severe drought across the Great Plains and Midwest in 1988 caused massive crop failures and water shortages. Yellowstone fires burned during this drought. Damage estimated at $40 billion.

properties.name: 1976 European Drought
properties.description: The summer of 1976 brought an exceptional drought to Western Europe, particularly the UK and France. Rivers dried up, crops failed, and the UK appointed a Minister for Drought.

properties.name: Afghanistan Drought
properties.description: Severe multi-year drought from 1998 to 2001 devastated Afghanistan's agriculture and water supply, displacing millions and compounding humanitarian crisis alongside conflict.

properties.name: 2014-15 Southeast Brazil Drought
properties.region: São Paulo, Minas Gerais, Rio de Janeiro
properties.severity: Worst in 80 years — São Paulo water crisis
properties.description: Severe drought struck southeastern Brazil from 2014 to 2015, threatening São Paulo's water supply for its 20 million residents. The Cantareira reservoir system fell below 5% capacity.

properties.name: Storm Daniel Greece Floods
properties.severity: Worst in Greek history — year's rain in hours
properties.description: Storm Daniel brought record rainfall to Thessaly in September 2023 before moving to Libya. Over 750mm fell in 18 hours, flooding the entire Thessaly plain and destroying crops.

properties.name: 2007 United Kingdom Floods
properties.description: Prolonged heavy rainfall in June and July 2007 caused widespread flooding across England, particularly in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and Gloucestershire. Over 55,000 properties were flooded.

properties.name: Vargas Tragedy Floods
properties.severity: Catastrophic — worst natural disaster in Venezuelan history
properties.description: Torrential rains in December 1999 triggered massive landslides and flash floods along Venezuela's Caribbean coast, destroying entire towns. Up to 30,000 people may have died.
